course_title: Social Pharmacy and the Health System 2A

learning_outcomes.description: Explore how to manage and lead change in a complex health environment.
Characterise and debate what it means for a pharmacist to apply social and ethical accountability for providing patient-centred, culturally safe care and services, and respond to health inequity.
Examine the responsibilities of pharmacists as health professionals working in a business environment.
Apply strategies and approaches that develop culturally safe practice, including recognition of the distinct needs of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.
Apply social and behavioural science regarding consumer behaviour, health, and illness to pharmacy practice.

assessments.task_description:
Students will complete two (2) online Cultural Safety in Practice modules throughout the semester and submit a reflection on their learning in each module:    

  • Cultural Safety in Practice Module 3: Cultural Safety in Health and Community Services 
  • Cultural Safety in Practice Module 4: Reflection on Professional Practice 

See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for more details of the assessment and the marking criteria.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Upload the assessment items to ePortfolio via the link provided in Learn.UQ (Blackboard).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: A/V Assignment : Response to a Complex Workplace Scenario
assessments.task_description:
This task involves providing an individual audio-video response to questions around behaviour. This scenario is taken from a workplace, and you are required to develop a management strategy based on patient-centred care.

  • Propose behavioural/motivational techniques that could be used to motivate the patient to change their behaviour and improve the health outcome. 
  • Record up to a 5-minute video of yourself explaining your response to the scenario questions.
  • There are 4 questions to be answered.
  • Your face must be visible throughout the video.
  • See Learn.UQ for more information and the marking criteria.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit a video file via the link provided in Learn.UQ (Blackboard).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This is an invigilated exam using the Inspera platform that students will complete using their own device. This assessment task is to be completed in person.

This is an MCQ examination marked out of 60.

It is a ONE hour exam with 10 min perusal in examination period.

The exam will be undertaken using your own laptop computer via the Inspera eAssessment platform (as will any deferred or supplementary written exams). Go to the Inspera Assessment Information page, which provides further information on: how to access Inspera, on-campus invigilated exams in Inspera, device requirements, familiarisation opportunities, and submitting assessments.

Link for Inspera Assessment Information page: Inspera Assessment (Ultra) - eLearning - University of Queensland
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

Independent learning via UQ Extend

Complete the active online learning via UQ Extend before attending the workshop.

Module 1: Social and Ethical Accountability I (week 1)

Module 2: Social and Ethical Accountability II (Week 2)

Module 3: Behavioural Change I (Week 3)

Module 4: Behavioural Change II (Week 4)

Module 5: Behavioural Change III (Week 6)

Module 6: Business Skills I (Week 7)

Module 7: Business Skills II (Week 8)

Module 8: Manage and Lead Change I (Week 9)

Module 9: Manage and Lead Change II (Week 10)

Module 10: Manage and Lead Change III (Week 11)

Module 11: Manage and Lead Change IV (Week 12)



Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
